_instructions: Assign publications to projects by adding project IDs to the 'projects' array. Publications are fetched automatically from ORCID, this file just tags them.

publications.title: Implications for oceanographic and seafloor geodetic applications due to settling of self-calibrating bottom pressure recorders
Decoding drivers of carbon flux attenuation in the oceanic biological pump
Assessment of oceanographic conditions during the North Atlantic EXport processes in the ocean from RemoTe Sensing (EXPORTS) Field campaign
Vertical imbalance in organic carbon budgets is indicative of a missing vertical transfer during a phytoplankton bloom near South Georgia (COMICS)
A seasonal transition in biological carbon pump efficiency in the northern Scotia Sea, Southern Ocean
Optical particle measurements reveal cross-shelf turbidity gradients on the Agulhas Bank
Primary production dynamics on the Agulhas Bank in autumn
Combining gliders and models to understand mesoscale biogeochemical patterns at the Angola-Benguela front
FIRe glider: Mapping in situ chlorophyll variable fluorescence with autonomous underwater gliders
Testing the Canyon Hypothesis: Evaluating light and nutrient controls of phytoplankton growth in penguin foraging hotspots along the West Antarctic Peninsula
Evaluating the sensor-equipped autonomous surface vehicle C-Worker 4 as a tool for identifying coastal ocean acidification
Coccolithovirus facilitation of carbon export in the North Atlantic
Coupled Physical and Phytoplankton dynamics in Coastal Antarctica
Mapping Antarctic phytoplankton physiology using autonomous gliders
Population structure and reproduction of Pseudione elongata africana (Bopyridae, Isopoda)
The Trans-Atlantic Slocum Glider Expeditions: A Catalyst for Undergraduate Participation in Ocean Science and Technology
Morphology of fish eggs: a comparative method for different salinities and preservatives

_how_to_use: For each publication above, add the relevant project IDs to the 'projects' array. Example: 'projects': ['rebels', 'partitrics'] if a publication relates to both projects. The ORCID integration will automatically fetch publications and match them by DOI or title.
